perturbation.original_text: 1.21 "Products" means the ENERGOUS products set forth in Exhibit A, as such Exhibit may be amended from time to time by mutual agreement between the parties, that have been released by ENERGOUS to production, including all Product Updates, which will be deemed to have been added to Exhibit A automatically, without any further action required by the parties, immediately following the release to production date.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.21 "Products" means the ENERGOUS products set forth in Exhibit A, as such Exhibit may be amended from time to time at ENERGOUS's sole discretion. All products must be mutually agreed upon and released by ENERGOUS to production. Product Updates will not automatically be deemed to be added to Exhibit A, and any further action is required by the parties, following the release to production date.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty in the definition of 'Products'. The original text implied that Product Updates are automatically included, but the changed text requires further action by the parties. This directly contradicts the automatic inclusion, creating potential disagreement about whether DIALOG has the right to manufacture and sell specific Product Updates. This makes it unclear if all the Energoous products are actually approved.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 License Grant. Subject to the restrictions set out in Section 2.2, ENERGOUS hereby grants to DIALOG a non-exclusive (subject to Section 2.5), irrevocable, worldwide, sub-licensable (solely in accordance with Section 2.4), royalty-bearing license during the Term under all Product IP to: (a) repackage or have repackaged the Product Die into various package formats or layouts, and to integrate the Product Die into MCMs, which may incorporate DIALOG or third party intellectual property (such repackaged Product Die, MCMs and Products, are individually and/or collectively referred to as the "Licensed Products"); (b) have the Licensed Products manufactured, tested and packaged by Manufacturing Subcontractors; (c) Sell, offer for Sale, import, export and support the Licensed Products, including without limitation, providing system design, troubleshooting and failure analysis support for DIALOG's customers and their customers; (d) use and modify the Tooling and Documentation for the purposes of paragraphs (a) to (d) of this Section 2.1.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the definition of 'Products' by stating that Product Updates will not automatically be added to Exhibit A, requiring further action by the parties. This directly contradicts the original text's implication that Product Updates are automatically included in Exhibit A without further action. This contradicts section 2.1 which grants DIALOG a license under all Product IP since it is now unclear which products and product updates are included.

perturbation.original_text: 11.2 Product Warranty. (a) ENERGOUS warrants that (i) when manufactured in accordance with the Approved Production Specifications, and as implemented in a suitable circuit application in accordance with the Product Specifications, the Products and Product Die will conform to the Product Specifications and will be free from defects that could have been avoided in their design; (ii) the Products, any constituent parts or functionality thereof, the Documentation and the Deposit Materials do not infringe any third party's Intellectual Property Rights; (iii) it did not misappropriate any third party's trade secrets in the development of the Products, any constituent parts or functionality thereof, the Documentation or the Deposit Materials; and (iv) when delivered (including any software updates if any), no Product will contain any viruses, "Trojan horses" or other harmful code. The above warranties are valid for a period of [***] from the date of shipment of any Licensed Product to any customer.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.2 Product Warranty. (a) ENERGOUS warrants that (i) when manufactured in accordance with the Approved Production Specifications, and as implemented in a suitable circuit application in accordance with the Product Specifications, the Products and Product Die will conform to a baseline Product Specifications document, however, there is no guarantee that it will be free from defects; (ii) ENERGOUS makes no claims that the Products, any constituent parts or functionality thereof, the Documentation and the Deposit Materials do not infringe any third party's Intellectual Property Rights; (iii) ENERGOUS does not guarantee that it did not misappropriate any third party's trade secrets in the development of the Products, any constituent parts or functionality thereof, the Documentation or the Deposit Materials; and (iv) ENERGOUS only uses their best reasonable effort, for delivered Products, any software updates, to not contain any viruses, "Trojan horses" or other harmful code. The above warranties are valid for a period of thirty (30) days from the date of shipment of any Licensed Product to any customer.
perturbation.explanation: This modifies the warranty ENERGOUS provides for the products. By changing the warranty to be a 'baseline' with 'no guarantee' to have no defects, and 'makes no claims' to the Intellectual Property Rights, this is contradicting the original, where it did guarantee these aspects, thus increasing liability of the DIALOG on the IP. As well as the change of 30 days to the original [***] time frame warranty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.1 Indemnification by Energous. Subject to Section 12.2, ENERGOUS agrees to indemnify, hold harmless and, in the case of any third party claims, defend DIALOG and its Affiliates and each of their respective directors, officers, employees, contractors, agents, distributors and customers (collectively, "DIALOG Indemnitees") from and against and in respect of any and all alleged or actual demands, claims, actions, causes of action, suits or proceedings, assessments, awarded damages (including punitive damages), liabilities, interest and penalties, costs and expenses (including, without limitation, court costs and reasonable legal fees and disbursements in connection therewith) (each, a "Claim") to the extent resulting from, arising out of, relating to, or imposed upon or incurred by any DIALOG Indemnitees by reason of (a) death or bodily injury caused by or resulting from use of the Products, (b) any breach of any representation or warranty made by ENERGOUS hereunder or to any third party in relation to the Products or Product Die, (c) the infringement or misappropriation of any third party Intellectual Property Rights in relation to the Products or Product Die, (d) the infringement or misappropriation of any third party Intellectual Property Rights as a result of DIALOG's or its Affiliates' exercise of rights in accordance with the terms of this Agreement, including, but not limited to, the Manufacturing Subcontractors' manufacture of the Products on their behalf, provided that the Products are manufactured in strict compliance with the Product Specifications and Approved Production Specifications and only to the extent such Claims arise due to compliance with the Product Specifications and/or the Approved Production Specifications or use of the Tooling provided by ENERGOUS hereunder, (e) the infringement by DIALOG of any third party Marks rights as a result of its authorized use of the ENERGOUS Marks, (f) any failure by ENERGOUS to comply with applicable laws, regulations and standards, or (g) ENERGOUS' negligence, intentional misconduct or fraud.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the warranties provided by ENERGOUS. The original text warrants that the Products will conform to Product Specifications and be free from defects, will not infringe on third-party IP rights, and will be free from harmful code. The changed text alters these warranties to a 'baseline' Product Specification with 'no guarantee' to have no defects, makes 'no claims' regarding IP rights infringement, doesn't guarantee against misappropriation of trade secrets, and uses 'best reasonable effort' regarding harmful code. This directly contradicts Section 12.1, which states that ENERGOUS will indemnify DIALOG for any claims resulting from a breach of ENERGOUS' warranties or infringement/misappropriation of IP rights. The weakened warranties in the changed text increase DIALOG's potential liability and create a strong contradiction with the indemnification clause, making the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 15.1 Term. This Agreement is effective on the Effective Date. Unless earlier terminated as provided herein, this Agreement continues in effect for an initial term of seven (7) years ("Initial Term") and will automatically renew for one or more annual periods after the Initial Term (each a "Renewal Term") unless either party gives notice of non-renewal at least one hundred eighty (180) days prior to the beginning of any Renewal Term.
perturbation.changed_text: 15.1 Term. This Agreement is effective on the Effective Date. Unless earlier terminated as provided herein, this Agreement continues in effect for an initial term of seven (7) years ("Initial Term") and will renew with a notice, by only ENERGOUS' descretion to make the renewal and DIALOG will continue on an annual periods after the Initial Term (each a "Renewal Term"). DIALOG are to give notice of non-renewal at least one hundred eighty (180) days prior to the beginning of any Renewal Term.
perturbation.explanation: The change in text makes the renewal agreement, a clause just for Energous by making it their descretion to make the renewal, by continuing the agreement or the Wind Down period with DIALOG. By also making the DIALOG give 180 days notice of non-renewal, this is not fair between parties in the contract, since Energous has the renewal clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 20.8 Construction. The parties and their respective counsel have negotiated this Agreement. This Agreement will be fairly interpreted in accordance with its terms and without any strict construction in favor of or against either party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text gives ENERGOUS sole discretion to renew the agreement while requiring DIALOG to provide 180 days' notice of non-renewal. This directly contradicts section 20.8, which states the agreement will be fairly interpreted without strict construction in favor of or against either party. The modification introduces a clear bias in favor of ENERGOUS, making the agreement unfair as the renewal clause is only for ENERGOUS, contradicting the principle of fair interpretation.
